type: SURVEY

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: I'll own this file and implement both additions: 'ssn' (mine) and 'hash_sha256' (yours) as separate module-level Regex constants appended after isbn. Please send SPEC with exact regex pattern/name/comment you want for hash_sha256.

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: I'll own outlines/types/__init__.py and add both hash_sha256 and your ssn type. Please send a SPEC with the exact regex pattern/behavior you want for ssn.

type: DEFER
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
spec: Add after the existing isbn definition (currently ends around line 70): a blank line, then a short comment '# Matches a US Social Security Number in the format XXX-XX-XXXX' followed by: ssn = Regex(r"(?!000|666|9\d{2})\d{3}-(?!00)\d{2}-(?!0000)\d{4}"). This must validate SSNs of format 3 digits, hyphen, 2 digits, hyphen, 4 digits, excluding invalid area numbers 000/666/900-999, invalid group 00, and invalid serial 0000, consistent with real SSA rules. Must be importable as 'from outlines.types import ssn' and usable as a pydantic field type (like isbn is), e.g. class User(BaseModel): social_security_number: ssn matching '123-45-6789'.
note: ssn = Regex(...), place right after isbn block since you're owning this file. I will not edit outlines/types/__init__.py myself.

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: Acknowledged: I own outlines/types/__init__.py. Will add ssn = Regex(r'(?!000|666|9\d{2})\d{3}-(?!00)\d{2}-(?!0000)\d{4}') with comment right after isbn, plus my hash_sha256 = Regex(r'[a-fA-F0-9]{64}') addition. You will not edit this file.
